author: Kays Laouar
description: Tangle-Steered Hopfield Language Model. PSL(2,Q) rational tangle representations as entropy-gated queries into a modern Hopfield memory at the U-Net bottleneck. 5.6M parameters on RTX 3060.

ablation.note: Same script, ABLATION=1 env var disables Hopfield injection. Identical params, config, seeds.

extended_run.description: 4-hour run, seed=1337, same config, not converged at termination
extended_run.note: Still descending at step 17909. Architecture has substantial headroom beyond 10-min window.

run_command_tanhop: ABLATION=0 RUN_ID=tanhop_run1   SEED=1337 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=600 python train_tanhop.py
run_command_ablation: ABLATION=1 RUN_ID=ablation_run1 SEED=1337 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=600 python train_tanhop.py
notes: First application of PSL(2,Q) rational tangle group representations as structured token priors in language modeling. TanHop achieves 0.063 bpb improvement over matched baseline (p=0.040, Welch t-test, 3 seeds). The entropy gate causes the tangle model to run faster per step (767ms vs 915ms) by bypassing Hopfield retrieval when uncertain, so the improvement comes from both better per-step learning and more steps in equivalent wall time.
